metadata.name: cubic_pool
metadata.description: Cubic Pool DEX - Multi-pool DEX with virtual liquidity and leverage logic

instructions.name: add_liquidity
docs: Add proportional liquidity and mint BPT to the user.
* `token_amounts`     — per-token deposit amounts in pool order.

accounts.name: token_program
docs: Token program for the BPT mint (same one used for `bpt_mint`).
Per-token programs come via `remaining_accounts` because each
pool token may use a different program.

instructions.name: collect_protocol_fees
docs: Sweep `protocol_fees_owed` for every token to caller-supplied

instructions.name: debug_withdraw_liquidity
docs: updated. Once vaults are drained out-of-band the invariant math
is meaningless, so the pool MUST stay `pool_enabled = false`
forever. Re-enabling would let users swap/join against stale
state and extract value at LP / protocol expense.

instructions.name: disable_pool_admin
docs: Permanently renounce pool-admin on this pool. Single-step,

instructions.name: get_pool_info
docs: Read-only "view": emit a `PoolInfo` event with the pool's full
state. Intended for `simulate({ commitment: ... })` calls; not
useful as part of a signed transaction.
SDK helpers, frontend pool-detail pages, indexer health-check

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: Initialise a new `CubicPoolConfig`. Treasury wrapper passes the
Treasury PDA as both admin pubkeys; direct calls produce
* `protocol_admin`           — pubkey allowed to pause pools, set
update banned extensions, and rotate itself via the
* `default_protocol_fee_rate`— seeded onto every new pool under
Pool-admin (level-1) is per-pool — set automatically to the wallet

accounts.name: protocol_admin_treasury
docs: the entire validation — any account passed in that isn't that
exact PDA fails before the handler runs. The handler then
requires the `protocol_admin` argument to equal this account's
key, so on-chain `config.protocol_admin` can only ever be the

accounts.name: payer
docs: Pays the rent for `config`. Must be a `Signer`.

instructions.name: initialize_cubic_pool
docs: Initialise a fresh pool under `config`. Creates the pool PDA and
BPT mint, validates token mints / weights / virtual balances, and
into PDA seeds. Unique per config.
One mint account per token in `tokens` order.

accounts.name: config
docs: Parent governance config. Read-only — only its pubkey and the

accounts.name: pool
docs: New pool PDA. Seeded so multiple pools per config are possible

accounts.name: bpt_mint
docs: Brand-new BPT mint. Authority is the pool PDA, decimals fixed at
`token_program` account passed below — this only constrains BPT,

instructions.name: initialize_pool_alt
docs: Provision a per-pool Address Lookup Table so client SDKs can build
pools whose account count exceeds the legacy 1232-byte ceiling
* `recent_slot` — recent slot used by the ALT program to derive
the table address. Caller should pass
One transaction performs three CPIs into the ALT program:
The ALT becomes immutable forever — its rent (~0.005 SOL for a
existing prod pools), and `sdk` after `initialize_cubic_pool` on

accounts.name: pool
docs: Pool to provision the ALT for. Must have `lookup_table ==
Pubkey::default()`. The handler writes the new ALT address back

accounts.name: authority
docs: existing per-pool admin avoids inventing a new role). Pays both
the ALT rent and the per-extend rent top-ups.

accounts.name: lookup_table
docs: `[authority, recent_slot]` by the ALT program. Re-derived and
matched against this account in the handler.

instructions.name: migrate_pool_v4
docs: Rewrite a v3-layout pool account into the v4 AoS layout. One-shot,
idempotent, signed by `pool.pool_admin`. Required exactly once per
pre-existing pool after the cubic-pool program is upgraded to a
binary built against the v4 struct.
`scripts/ping-prod-pools.ts` reports which pools need this; the
admin runs it as a follow-up. New pools created via

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal the v3 `pool_admin` field stored inside `pool.data`.
Pool-admin only; protocol-admin can't trigger this — the storage

instructions.name: range_manager_update
docs: Tweak any subset of `virtual_balances` and / or `normalized_weights`
within the configured envelope. Range-manager only.
empty. The resulting weight vector must sum to `WEIGHT_SCALE`
and stay within `[MIN_WEIGHT, MAX_WEIGHT]` per token, otherwise

accounts.name: pool
docs: Mutated — handler writes new virtual_balances / normalized_weights

instructions.name: remove_liquidity
docs: Burn BPT proportionally and pay out tokens.
The pool always retains at least `MINIMUM_INITIAL_BPT` of supply
so the proportional math never sees a zero-supply state. If the
caller's `bpt_amount` would push supply below the floor, the burn
is silently capped at `total_supply - MINIMUM_INITIAL_BPT` and
the residual BPT stays in the user's wallet.

instructions.name: set_banned_extensions
docs: Update the Token-2022 banned-extensions bitmap on a config.

instructions.name: set_max_selloff
docs: token in a single call. Pool-admin only.
disables the check on that token.

instructions.name: set_pool_enabled
docs: Master pool kill-switch. When `enabled = false`, swaps and

instructions.name: set_range_manager
docs: Set or disable the range-manager pubkey on `pool`. Pool-admin only —
Pubkey::default()` with `enabled = false` for the fully-disabled
state. New pools deploy with this off by default.
* `enabled`     — gate flag; `false` blocks the manager even if

instructions.name: set_range_manager_config
docs: Configure the envelope the range-manager must stay within. Pool-
admin only. Limits are per-token (applied independently in the
update loop) and basis points of the CURRENT value.

instructions.name: swap
docs: Swap one pool token for another using `calc_out_given_in`.

accounts.name: user_token_account_in
docs: User's input-token account. Mint and owner cross-checked.

accounts.name: vault_in
docs: Pool input vault. ATA(pool, mint_in, token_program_in) — verified

errors.name: InvalidTokenCount
errors.msg: Invalid token count. Must be between 2 and 10

errors.name: InvalidTokenIndex
errors.msg: Invalid token index

errors.name: InvalidWeights
errors.msg: Invalid weights. Must sum to 10000 (WEIGHT_SCALE)

errors.name: InvalidVirtualBalances
errors.msg: Invalid virtual balances. Must be greater than zero

errors.msg: Insufficient liquidity in the pool

errors.msg: Slippage tolerance exceeded

errors.msg: Invalid amounts provided

errors.name: InsufficientBptOut
errors.msg: Insufficient BPT minted

errors.name: InsufficientTokensOut
errors.msg: Insufficient tokens received

errors.name: FeeRateMaxExceeded
errors.msg: Fee rate exceeds maximum

errors.name: ProtocolFeeRateMaxExceeded
errors.msg: Protocol fee rate exceeds maximum

errors.msg: Math overflow

errors.name: MathUnderflow
errors.msg: Math underflow

errors.name: DivisionByZero
errors.msg: Division by zero

errors.name: TokenMintMismatch
errors.msg: Token mint mismatch

errors.name: InvalidTokenDecimals
errors.msg: Invalid token decimals

errors.name: AmountOutExceedsBalance
errors.msg: Amount out exceeds available balance

errors.name: InvalidBptAmount
errors.msg: Invalid BPT amount

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Unauthorized

errors.name: InvalidMint
errors.msg: Invalid mint account

errors.name: PoolDisabled
errors.msg: Pool is disabled. No operations allowed.

errors.name: SwapsDisabled
errors.msg: Swaps are disabled for this pool.

errors.name: PoolMustBeDisabled
errors.msg: Pool must be disabled before debug operations.

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: ZeroFeeAmount
errors.msg: Swap amount is too small: fee rounds to zero

errors.name: BannedExtension
errors.msg: Token mint has a banned Token-2022 extension

errors.name: TokenProgramMismatch
errors.msg: Token program does not match pool's token program

errors.name: UserTokenAccountOwnerMismatch
errors.msg: User token account owner mismatch

errors.name: InitialLiquidityTooSmall
errors.msg: Initial liquidity too small - pool would be bricked

errors.name: InvalidTokenProgram
errors.msg: Invalid token program - must be SPL Token or Token-2022

errors.name: InvalidVault
errors.msg: Invalid vault account

errors.name: InvalidSourceOwner
errors.msg: Source account must be owned by this program

errors.name: WouldBreakRentExempt
errors.msg: Withdrawal would break rent exemption

errors.name: PoolAdminDisabled
errors.msg: Pool admin is disabled

errors.name: ProtocolAdminUnset
errors.msg: Protocol admin is unset

errors.name: NoPendingAdmin
errors.msg: No pending admin transfer

errors.name: NotPendingAdmin
errors.msg: Signer is not the pending admin

errors.name: InvalidPendingAdmin
errors.msg: Pending admin cannot be the zero pubkey

errors.name: ProtocolAdminNotTreasury
errors.msg: protocol_admin must equal the hardcoded Treasury PDA

errors.name: RangeManagerDisabled
errors.msg: Range manager is not enabled for this pool

errors.name: RangeManagerUnauthorized
errors.msg: Signer is not the configured range manager

errors.name: RangeManagerUpdateTooFrequent
errors.msg: Range manager update too frequent — minimum interval not yet elapsed

errors.name: RangeManagerVbChangeTooLarge
errors.msg: Range manager virtual balance change exceeds configured bps cap

errors.name: RangeManagerWeightChangeTooLarge
errors.msg: Range manager weight change exceeds configured bps cap

errors.name: RangeManagerEmptyUpdate
errors.msg: Range manager update must change at least one virtual balance or weight

errors.name: RangeManagerInvalidBps
errors.msg: Range manager bps parameter exceeds 10000

errors.name: RangeManagerWeightSumInvalid
errors.msg: Resulting weights do not sum to WEIGHT_SCALE after range manager update

errors.name: RangeManagerDuplicateIndex
errors.msg: Duplicate token index in range manager update

errors.name: MaxSelloffInvalidConfig
errors.msg: max_selloff_period_length must be > 0 when max_selloff > 0

errors.name: MaxSelloffExceeded
errors.msg: Token max-selloff threshold exceeded for current window

errors.name: AlreadyMigrated
errors.msg: Pool is already migrated to v4

errors.name: UnexpectedPoolSize
errors.msg: Unexpected pool account size — not v3 or v4

errors.name: InvalidPoolDiscriminator
errors.msg: Account discriminator does not match CubicPool

errors.name: AltAlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Pool already has an Address Lookup Table initialised

errors.name: AltAddressMismatch
errors.msg: ALT address mismatch — re-derived from recent_slot doesn't match passed account

errors.name: InvalidAltProgram
errors.msg: Invalid Address Lookup Table program id

types.name: AssetConfig
docs: `CubicPool` v4 — per-pool state for a weighted AMM with virtual liquidity.
Storage layout: **Array-of-Structs (AoS)**. Per-token data lives in
This mirrors Solend / MarginFi / Kamino's `Reserve { config, ... }`
pattern. Reading per-token data is a single struct lookup; auditors
see at a glance which fields a user can mutate vs which need pool
code both read fields by raw offset. Adding fields is OK if appended
at the END (before the trailing `reserved`) and `reserved` shrinks
accordingly. Inserting in the middle requires a new migration.
- `token_decimals`  — read from each mint account at runtime.
v3 stored per-token data as parallel arrays (`token_mints[10]`,

fields.name: mint
docs: Token mint pubkey for this slot. `Pubkey::default()` past `token_count`.

fields.name: token_program
docs: SPL Token or Token-2022 program ID for this token's vault/transfers.

fields.name: normalized_weight
docs: Normalised weight in basis points (sum across active slots == 10_000).

fields.name: max_selloff
docs: Max cumulative sell volume of this token (as `amount_in` in swaps)
the check for this token. Set by pool_admin only.

fields.name: max_selloff_period_length
docs: Length of the sliding window in seconds. Ignored when

fields.name: virtual_balance
docs: Virtual balance (raw token units) used by the cubic invariant
math. Decoupled from `actual_balance` to enable concentrated
liquidity. Updated by swap / add_liquidity / remove_liquidity.

fields.name: actual_balance
docs: Mirrors the on-chain vault balance for this token. Always equals
the vault's `amount` after every successful tx (CEI ordering

fields.name: protocol_fees_owed
docs: Accumulated protocol fees for this token (raw units), awaiting
so LP value isn't diluted by uncollected fees.

fields.name: previous_selloff
docs: Sliding-window rate limiter: amount sold in the PREVIOUS window

fields.name: current_selloff
docs: Amount sold in the CURRENT window.

fields.name: window_start_timestamp
docs: `Clock::unix_timestamp` when the current window started. New
pools: set to `now()` at init. After v3→v4 migration: set to

types.name: BannedExtensionsUpdated
docs: Emitted by `set_banned_extensions`. Off-chain validators use this to
refresh the cached blocklist without rereading the config account.

fields.name: config
docs: `CubicPoolConfig` this pool inherits governance from. Pinned at
init, used both as a PDA seed and at runtime in admin-instruction

fields.name: bump
docs: Bump seed for the pool PDA. Stored so signer-seed reconstruction is
O(1) instead of an expensive `find_program_address` per CPI.

fields.name: token_count
docs: Number of active tokens in the pool, in [MIN_TOKENS, MAX_TOKENS] =
[2, 10]. Slots beyond `token_count` are zeroed and ignored.

fields.name: pool_id
docs: User-chosen 64-bit salt baked into the PDA seeds, allowing multiple
pools per config without re-deploying. Unique per (config, pool_id).

fields.name: swap_fee_rate
docs: Swap fee in hundredths of a basis point (precision

fields.name: protocol_fee_rate
docs: Share of each swap fee taken by the protocol, in basis points

fields.name: pool_enabled
docs: Master kill switch — when `false`, swaps and add/remove liquidity
all revert. Only `protocol_admin` can flip this.

fields.name: swaps_enabled
docs: Trading switch — `false` blocks swaps but still allows LP

fields.name: pool_admin
docs: Per-pool admin. Owns: rotate swap_fee_rate, toggle swaps_enabled,
set range_manager, set max_selloff, transfer/disable itself.

fields.name: pending_pool_admin
docs: Pending successor for `pool_admin` during a 2-step transfer.

fields.name: tokens
docs: All per-token state. Slot `i` is for the i-th token. Slots past

fields.name: lookup_table
docs: Per-pool Address Lookup Table address. Holds pool-scoped accounts
(pool PDA, BPT mint, vaults, mints, token programs) so client SDKs
without hitting the 1232-byte legacy-tx ceiling on multi-token pools.
`Pubkey::default()` ⇒ ALT not yet initialised. SDK falls back to
legacy tx (will only work for small pools). Set by `initialize_pool_alt`,
frozen on-chain so depositors don't worry about authority abuse.

types.name: CubicPoolConfig
docs: `CubicPoolConfig` — protocol-level governance for every pool created
One config can host many pools (each pool stores `config: Pubkey`).
lives on `CubicPool::pool_admin`. This config carries only the

fields.name: protocol_admin
docs: Protocol-level admin. Allowed to: pause pools, change
SOL from program-owned accounts, and rotate itself via the
pending/accept flow. Set to a designated authority (typically the
Treasury PDA of the `protocol-admin` program) at config init.

fields.name: pending_protocol_admin
docs: Pending successor for `protocol_admin` during a 2-step transfer.

fields.name: default_protocol_fee_rate
docs: onto every new pool created against this config. Pools may diverge

fields.name: banned_extensions
docs: Bitmap of banned Token-2022 extension types. Bit N set ⇒ extension
type N is rejected at pool init. Maintained by `protocol_admin` via

fields.name: reserved
docs: Forward-compatibility padding so future fields can be added without

types.name: DebugLiquidityWithdrawn
docs: Emitted by `debug_withdraw_liquidity`. Listening for this event is the
canonical way for off-chain monitors to know that a pool has
`virtual_balances` no longer match vault holdings after this fires.

types.name: LiquidityAdded
docs: Emitted by `add_liquidity` after BPT is minted.
* `token_amounts` — what the user actually deposited (raw units, in

types.name: LiquidityRemoved
docs: Emitted by `remove_liquidity` after BPT is burned. `bpt_amount` is the
not the original request — clients reading this know exactly how much

types.name: MaxSelloffSet
docs: `max_selloff_period_length` are the post-update arrays of length
`token_count`. A zero in `max_selloff[i]` means the check is

types.name: MaxSelloffWindowAdvanced
docs: Emitted from inside `swap` whenever the sliding-window check for

types.name: PoolAltInitialized
docs: Emitted by `initialize_pool_alt` once the ALT is created, extended
with all pool-scoped addresses, and frozen. Off-chain SDK / indexer
uses this as the signal that v0 transactions are usable for this pool.

fields.name: authority
docs: Wallet that authored creation (matches `pool.pool_admin` at the
time of the call). After freeze the ALT has no authority.

fields.name: address_count
docs: Number of addresses written into the ALT.

types.name: PoolInfo
docs: Read-only view event emitted by the simulation-only `get_pool_info`
and parse this event from the returned logs. Strictly more verbose

types.name: PoolInitialized
docs: Emitted at the end of `initialize_cubic_pool`. Marks a new pool live.

types.name: PoolMigratedToV4
docs: Emitted by `migrate_pool_v4` after a successful v3 → v4 storage
reorganization. Off-chain monitors should treat this as the
"this specific pool is now safe to interact with at v4" signal.

types.name: PoolSolWithdrawn
docs: Emitted by `withdraw_sol` when SOL is recovered from a cubic-pool-owned

types.name: PoolStateLog
docs: Mutating-instruction state snapshot consumed by the backend indexer to
keep its denormalised pool table in sync without an additional account
`bpt_total_supply` is the post-tx supply (0 for `swap`, which does not
touch supply — clients should fall back to the mint account).

types.name: ProtocolFeesCollected
docs: Emitted by `collect_protocol_fees` once per call (one event covers all
tokens, with zero-amounts kept in `token_amounts` to preserve order).

types.name: RangeManagerConfigSet
docs: Emitted by `set_range_manager_config`. Carries the full effective
envelope so off-chain indexers don't need to re-fetch the pool.

types.name: RangeManagerSet
docs: Emitted by `set_range_manager`. Both the pubkey and the enabled flag
are bundled into a single event. `new_manager == Pubkey::default()`
AND `enabled = false` is the "fully disabled" state.

types.name: RangeManagerUpdated
docs: Emitted by `range_manager_update` after every check passes and state

types.name: Swap
docs: Emitted by `swap` after the trade executes.
* `token_in/out`       — mint pubkeys of the two legs
* `amount_in/out`      — raw token units (pre-fee on input side)
* `fee_amount`         — total swap fee (raw units, in token_in)
* `protocol_fee_amount`— share of `fee_amount` that went to the

types.name: SwapFeeRateUpdated
docs: Emitted by `set_swap_fee_rate`. Carries both old and new for cheap
audit reconstruction without re-reading prior state.

types.name: TokenChange
docs: Single (token_index, new_value) entry. Used by both the
